---
title: "WGCNA for leaf in control, consensus test for four lines. Part I - data input".
format: html
date: "2026-04-17"
execute:
  fig-format: png
  fig.dpi: 300
---

Built with R version `r getRversion()`

# Rationale

9 timepoints are available per line. There are 3 biological replications so in all we have 27 samples per line.
"time of day" (three letters) * "day" (1 to 3) * "biological replication" (1 to 3) $3 * 3 * 3 = 27$ samples.
The sample size is somewhat less than mentioned in FAQ ('at least 30 per category') but is near and I'll check the modules with that found in 'curve' (experiment 2) - control with more samples (**to do**).

The overall aim is to perform consensus WGCNA - separate per line and jointly.

The aim of this script is to prepare, filter and format data for WGCNA.

# Data preparation

## Environment cleaning and set-up

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
# load required packages
library(GenomicRanges)
library(WGCNA)
library(dplyr)
library(DESeq2)
```


```{r instal-packages}
#| eval: false
#| output: false
#| message: false
# Clean working directory, if needed
rm(list=ls())

if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
    install.packages("BiocManager")
BiocManager::install("impute")
BiocManager::install("preprocessCore")
BiocManager::install("GO.db")
BiocManager::install("AnnotationDbi")

install.packages("WGCNA")
```

:::{.callout-important}
Peter Langfelder doesn't advise filtering on variance (In `DESEq2`?) - [bioconductor forum](https://support.bioconductor.org/p/127855/#127864)
:::

Subsetting before or after vst? I'll try after, as count filtering must be done on raw counts.

## Read-in data

```{r read data, cache=TRUE}
load("../../../input-data/rdata/counts_and_metadata.RDa")

# previously prepared range data
load("../../../input-data/rdata/ranges.RDa")

# Check if files are in concert
all(rownames(coldata) %in% colnames(cts))
all(rownames(coldata) == colnames(cts))
```

## Subset leaf data

Subsetting is done at the level of raw data (counts and coldata).
```{r subset leaf data}
# subset coldata
coldata.leaf=subset(coldata, tissue=="leaf")

# check
dim(coldata.leaf)

# vector of sample names to keep - needed to select columns from count file
keep.leaf=rownames(coldata.leaf)

# check - only samples named "la5..." should remain
keep.leaf
length(keep.leaf)

# subsetting counts
cts.leaf=subset(cts, select=keep.leaf)

# check
dim(cts.leaf)
colnames(cts.leaf)

# Check if counts and coldata are in concert
all(rownames(coldata.leaf) %in% colnames(cts.leaf))
all(rownames(coldata.leaf) == colnames(cts.leaf))

keep = c("coldata.leaf", "cts.leaf", "gzakresy")

rm(list = setdiff(ls(), keep))
```

## Data preparation

DESeq data set must be created to perform VST.

```{r factors}
#| output: false
#| message: false

## Prepare grouping variable for use in model in dds

# add variable describing chronology of samples
coldata.leaf$timepoint = as.integer(as.numeric(coldata.leaf$time)) + ( as.integer(as.numeric(coldata.leaf$day)) - 1 ) * 3

# Adding grouping variable to easily analyse all comparisons needed
coldata.leaf$group <- factor(paste0(coldata.leaf$line, "_", coldata.leaf$timepoint))

# make factors - probably not needed

coldata.leaf <- coldata.leaf |>
  mutate(across(c(letter, tissue, line, time, day, rep, condition, Mread_bin, timepoint, group), as.factor))

# check
str(coldata.leaf)
```

## Create DEseq Data Set

```{r dds, cache=TRUE}
#| output: false
#| message: false

# create dataset, design included only for command completeness, different will be used in DE analysis
dds.leaf = DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = cts.leaf, colData = coldata.leaf, rowRanges = gzakresy, design = ~ group)
```

## Pre-filtering

:::{.callout-important}
According to FAQ provided with WGCNA tutorial:
> removing all features that have a count of less than say 10 in more than 90% of the samples.

Filtering of the whole dataset - not subset for each line.
:::

```{r filter, cache=TRUE}
# keeping genes having at least 10 counts in at least 90% samples
## what is 90% for all leaf samples?
perc90 = round(nrow(coldata.leaf) * 0.9, 0)

perc90

keep <- rowSums(counts(dds.leaf) >= 10) >= perc90
dds.leaf.f <- dds.leaf[keep,]

# check
dim(dds.leaf.f)

# what percent of genes remained?
100*(dim(dds.leaf.f)[1]/dim(dds.leaf)[1])

# 40.07 %
```

## Normalization

According to [post](https://www.biostars.org/p/459013/#459016) `varianceStabilizingTransformation` not `vst` is used. Design is provided here only because it is required in `dds` object. It makes no sense using `blind=FALSE` here.

```{r normalize, cache=TRUE}
vsd.leaf = varianceStabilizingTransformation(dds.leaf.f, blind=TRUE)

rm(list = setdiff(ls(), c('vsd.leaf', 'coldata.leaf')))
```

## Extract normalized counts

```{r extract normalized counts}
vsd.counts = assay(vsd.leaf)

coldata = colData(vsd.leaf)

# check
dim(vsd.counts)
```

The dataset must be splitted by factor compared in consensus analysis - here 'line'

```{r split}

for (i in unique(coldata$line)) {
  # subset coldata
  
  counts_name = paste0('vsd.counts4wgcna.', i)
  
  x = subset(coldata, line == i)
  keep=rownames(x)
  res=subset(vsd.counts, select=keep)
  print(dim(res))
  print(colnames(res))
  
  # transform to data frame and transpose
  res2 = as.data.frame(t(res))
  print(dim(res2))
  print(rownames(res2))
  
  assign(counts_name, res2, envir = .GlobalEnv)
}

```


# Consensus analysis

## Data input, cleaning & preprocessing


```{r}
#| output: false

options(stringsAsFactors = FALSE) # Important option, do not omit!
```

## Reformat data

```{r format data}
# We have 4 sets
nSets = 4

# For easier labeling of plots, create a vector holding descriptive names of the sets.
setLabels = c('a554', 's018', 's311', 's84')

# Form multi-set expression data
multiExpr = vector(mode = "list", length = nSets)

multiExpr[[1]] = list(data = vsd.counts4wgcna.a554)
multiExpr[[2]] = list(data = vsd.counts4wgcna.s018)
multiExpr[[3]] = list(data = vsd.counts4wgcna.s311)
multiExpr[[4]] = list(data = vsd.counts4wgcna.s84)

# Check that the data has the correct format for many functions operating on multiple sets:
exprSize = checkSets(multiExpr)
exprSize
```

## Checking data for excessive missing values and identification of outlier samples

```{r check missing}
# Check the data for missing values
gsg = goodSamplesGenesMS(multiExpr, verbose = 3)
gsg$allOK
```

We now cluster the samples on their Euclidean distance, separately in each set.

```{r cluster}
sampleTrees = list()
for (set in 1:nSets)
{
sampleTrees[[set]] = hclust(dist(multiExpr[[set]]$data), method = "average")
}
```

The easiest way to see the multiple dendrograms at the same time is to plot both into a pdf file that can be viewed
using standard pdf viewers.

```{r dendro}
pdf(file = "SampleClustering.pdf", width = 24, height = 12);
par(mfrow=c(2,2))
#par(mar = c(0, 4, 2, 0))
for (set in 1:nSets)
plot(sampleTrees[[set]], main = paste("Sample clustering on all genes in", setLabels[set]),
xlab="", sub="", cex = 0.7);
dev.off()
```

There are no outliers but samples are clustered by time of day.

# Loading category data

It is essentially coldata information. May be used in plots.

```{r cats}
# rename
allTraits = coldata.leaf

nSets = 4

Traits = vector(mode="list", length = nSets)

for (set in 1:nSets)
{
setSamples = rownames(multiExpr[[set]]$data)
traitRows = match(setSamples, rownames(allTraits))
Traits[[set]] = list(data = allTraits[traitRows, !(names(allTraits) %in% c('pool.plant', 'letter','tissue','pool.time','condition','id'))])
}
collectGarbage()

# Define data set dimensions
exprSize = checkSets(multiExpr)

nGenes = exprSize$nGenes
nSamples = exprSize$nSample
```

```{r save}
# dir for saved objects
dir.create("../rdata-saved/wgcna")
save(multiExpr, Traits, nGenes, nSamples, setLabels, exprSize, 
     file = "../rdata-saved/wgcna/Consensus-dataInput.RData")
```
